dataset: Synthea-derived patient database (20 base patients) + task-specific synthetic data

pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh both specify Elena. Patient does NOT pre-exist in the Synthea-loaded patients.sql (verified via live DB query: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM patient WHERE ptfname LIKE '%Elena%' AND ptlname LIKE '%Vasquez%' returned 0). This is a new patient to be created by the agent.
Task description and setup_task.sh both specify Vasquez-Moreno. Hyphenated last name is a realistic Hispanic naming convention. Not in the existing 20-patient Synthea database.

Task description says 'DOB: April 23, 1966'. This is synthetic data for a ~59-year-old patient, clinically consistent with the chronic disease profile (Type 2 DM, HTN). Consistent across task.json description and metadata.
Task description explicitly says 'Female'. FreeMED patient table uses ptsex field. Consistent across all task files.

Synthetic address. Beacon Street is a real street in Boston's Back Bay neighborhood (consistent with zip 02116). The specific number 457 and apartment 3B are synthetic but realistic for the Back Bay brownstone neighborhood. FreeMED stores this in ptaddr1 column.
Consistent with zip code 02116 which is confirmed as Boston, MA Back Bay area via USPS and unitedstateszipcodes.org.
Consistent with Boston city and 02116 zip code. Verified via USPS zip code lookup.
Verified via unitedstateszipcodes.org: 02116 is a valid USPS zip code for Boston, MA covering the Back Bay neighborhood. Consistent with Beacon Street address.
Synthetic phone number. 617 is a valid area code for Boston, MA. 555-xxxx numbers are the standard US fictional phone number prefix (reserved by NANPA for fictional use). Correctly synthetic.

This is a fictional insurance company name. It does NOT exist as a real insurer (web search confirms no real entity by this name). However, its address (101 Huntington Ave) and phone (800-262-2583) match the real Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts headquarters. The fictional name is intentional — setup_task.sh deletes any existing record with this name and requires the agent to create it fresh in the insco table.

Verified as a real address: 101 Huntington Avenue, Boston, MA 02199 is a 25-story Class A office building in the Prudential Center complex, headquarters of Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts. Source: BXP (Boston Properties) property listing.
Verified via BXP property listing for 101 Huntington Avenue.
Verified via BXP property listing for 101 Huntington Avenue.
Verified via unitedstateszipcodes.org: 02199 is a valid USPS zip code for the Prudential Center area of Boston, MA. Consistent with 101 Huntington Avenue address.
This is the real member services phone number for Blue Cross Blue Shield of Massachusetts (verified via bluecrossma.com). Used here with the fictional company name 'Northeastern Health Partners'.

Synthetic group number. Format is realistic (prefix + 5 digits). This is the value the agent must enter into the covpatgrpno field of the coverage table. No real-world reference to verify against — synthetic by design.

Synthetic policy number. Format is realistic (insurer prefix + number). 'NHP' prefix matches 'Northeastern Health Partners'. This is the value the agent must enter into the covpatinsno field. Synthetic by design.

Synthetic effective date. January 1 is a standard insurance plan effective date (annual enrollment). Stored in coveffdt (TEXT type) in the coverage table. Consistent across task description and metadata.

Verified via AAPC (aapc.com/codes/icd9-codes/250.00) and icd9data.com: ICD-9 250.00 = 'Diabetes mellitus without mention of complication, type II or unspecified type, not stated as uncontrolled.' This is the standard ICD-9 code used for Type 2 DM in clinical practice. Maps to ICD-10 E11.9. NOTE: FreeMED current_problems table stores diagnosis in a single 'problem' VARCHAR(250) field — there is no separate ICD code column. The code may appear within the problem text.

Verified via AAPC (aapc.com/codes/icd9-codes/401.9) and icd9data.com: ICD-9 401.9 = 'Unspecified essential hypertension.' This is the standard ICD-9 code for essential hypertension. Maps to ICD-10 I10. Same storage caveat as diabetes — stored in problem text field.

Clinically valid drug class. ACE inhibitor-induced angioedema is a well-documented adverse reaction (incidence ~0.1-0.2% of patients, per UpToDate and PMC review articles). FreeMED stores this in allergies_atomic.allergy (VARCHAR 150). The agent must enter this as the allergen name.

Verified via UpToDate and AAFP (aafp.org/pubs/afp/issues/2002/0915/p956.html): Angioedema is the hallmark adverse reaction to ACE inhibitors, caused by bradykinin accumulation. Stored in allergies_atomic.reaction (VARCHAR 150).
Clinically appropriate severity classification. Angioedema can be life-threatening (airway compromise), so 'severe' is the correct severity level. Stored in allergies_atomic.severity (VARCHAR 150).

Verified via StatPearls/NCBI (ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK526065/): Losartan is an ARB (Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker), FDA-approved for hypertension. It is the standard alternative when ACE inhibitors are contraindicated due to angioedema. Stored in rx.rxdrug (VARCHAR 150).
Verified via StatPearls: 50mg is the standard initial dose of Losartan for hypertension (range: 25-100mg daily). Stored in rx.rxdosage (VARCHAR 128).
30 tablets = 30-day supply at 1 tablet daily dosing. Standard prescription quantity for a monthly supply. Stored in rx.rxquantity (DOUBLE).
5 refills = 6 months total supply (initial fill + 5 refills). Standard for chronic medication management. Stored in rx.rxrefills (INT UNSIGNED).

Synthetic vital sign. 152 mmHg systolic = Stage 2 Hypertension per AHA/ACC guidelines (>=140 systolic). Clinically consistent with a patient who has 'Essential Hypertension' (ICD 401.9) and 'elevated blood pressure' per the task note requirements. Stored in vitals.v_bp_s_value (DOUBLE). Export maps to 'bp_systolic' key.
Synthetic vital sign. 94 mmHg diastolic = Stage 2 Hypertension per AHA/ACC (>=90 diastolic). Consistent with 152 systolic. Stored in vitals.v_bp_d_value (DOUBLE). Export maps to 'bp_diastolic' key.
Synthetic vital sign. 84 bpm = normal heart rate (60-100 bpm). Stored in vitals.v_pulse_value (DOUBLE). Export maps to 'heart_rate' key.
Normal body temperature (98.6F / 37.0C). Stored in vitals.v_temp_value (DOUBLE) with v_temp_units='F'. Export maps to 'temperature' key.

Synthetic vital sign. 178 lbs for a 5'4" female gives BMI ~30.5 (obese), clinically consistent with Type 2 DM risk profile. Stored in vitals.v_w_value (DOUBLE) with v_w_units='LB'. Export maps to 'weight' key.
Synthetic vital sign. 64 inches = 5'4", average height for a US female. Stored in vitals.v_h_value (DOUBLE) with v_h_units='IN'. Export maps to 'height' key.

Clinically appropriate: Endocrinology is the correct specialty for diabetes management referrals. FreeMED referrals table stores provider reference via refprovdest (user ID), not a text specialty field. The destination provider Dr. Anita Patel is pre-inserted into the user table with userdescrip='Endocrinologist' by setup_task.sh. Verified via live DB: user ID 2 = Anita Patel, type=phy, descrip=Endocrinologist.

Synthetic provider name. Dr. Anita Patel is inserted into the FreeMED user table by setup_task.sh (userfname='Anita', userlname='Patel', usertype='phy', usertitle='Dr.'). Verified via live DB query: user ID 2 exists with correct name and type. Export resolves refprovdest ID to provider name via JOIN on user table.
Synthetic referral date. Stored in referrals.refstamp (TIMESTAMP). Consistent between task description ('April 1, 2025') and metadata.

These are keywords expected in the clinical progress note. Each is clinically justified: 'diabetes' and 'hypertension' are the two diagnoses; 'ACE' and 'angioedema' relate to the allergy; 'losartan' is the prescribed medication; 'HbA1c' and 'lipid' are the lab orders mentioned in the care plan. Note: FreeMED pnotes table uses SOAP format (pnotes_S, pnotes_O, pnotes_A, pnotes_P, etc.) — export_result.sh concatenates all non-empty SOAP sections for keyword matching.

Synthetic follow-up date (~3 months after referral date of 2025-04-01). Clinically standard interval for chronic disease follow-up. Stored in scheduler.caldateof (DATE). Consistent between task description ('July 15, 2025') and metadata.
14 = 2:00 PM in 24-hour format. Consistent with task description ('2:00 PM'). Stored in scheduler.calhour (INT).
30-minute appointment. Standard duration for a follow-up visit in primary care. Stored in scheduler.calduration (INT). Consistent between task description ('30-minute follow-up') and metadata.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a new patient Elena Vasquez-Moreno (DOB 1966-04-23, Female, 457 Beacon Street Apt 3B, Boston MA 02116, phone 617-555-8294) who does not exist in the database at task start. The agent must add a fictional insurance company 'Northeastern Health Partners' at 101 Huntington Avenue, Boston MA 02199, phone 800-262-2583 (address/phone modeled after real Blue Cross Blue Shield of MA headquarters), then link coverage with group GRP-44821, policy NHP-998-7721, effective 2024-01-01. Two diagnoses must be added to the current_problems table: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (ICD-9 250.00, onset 2015-03-10) and Essential Hypertension (ICD-9 401.9, onset 2018-07-22). NOTE: the current_problems table has only a 'problem' text field (VARCHAR 250) with no separate ICD code column — codes may be embedded in the text. An allergy must be recorded in allergies_atomic: ACE Inhibitors, reaction angioedema, severity severe. A prescription for Losartan 50mg (qty 30, 5 refills) must be entered in the rx table (columns: rxdrug, rxdosage, rxquantity, rxrefills). Vitals must be recorded in the vitals table using actual column names: v_bp_s_value=152, v_bp_d_value=94, v_pulse_value=84, v_temp_value=98.6, v_w_value=178, v_h_value=64. A referral must target Dr. Anita Patel (user ID 2, pre-inserted as 'phy' type) in Endocrinology, stored in referrals table via refprovdest (user ID), refreasons (text), and refstamp. The clinical note uses FreeMED SOAP format (pnotes_S through pnotes_R columns) and must mention diabetes, hypertension, ACE, angioedema, losartan, HbA1c, and lipid. A 30-minute appointment must be scheduled for 2025-07-15 at hour 14 in the scheduler table (caldateof, calhour, calduration).
